name: Browser
description: A pane the user views. Preferred use: write a viewable file (.html/.md) in the cwd, then set 'source' to its path — the pane live-reloads it as you edit. 'source' also takes http(s) URLs (host-fetched). Read back what the user sees via the page state and snapshot outputs.

contextExports.label: Browser source
contextExports.description: PRIMARY input — set this to what the Browser pane should load: a file path relative to the conversation cwd (e.g. 'reports/summary.html') or an http(s) URL. Preferred flow: write your HTML/Markdown to a file, then set this to its path — no token cap, and the pane live-reloads the file on every edit. URLs are fetched once by the host (15s / 2MB caps, text only) and rendered without executing their scripts; setting the same URL again refreshes it.

contextExports.label: Browser content
contextExports.description: Direct-push fallback for SMALL, self-contained HTML or Markdown fragments (hard cap ~8k tokens; each push spends that from context). For anything substantial, write a file and set 'source' to its path instead. If the value starts with '<' it is treated as HTML, otherwise as Markdown.
